title: JOBY Stock Climbs As New York eVTOL Flights Impress Wall Street
text: Joby Aviation stock has climbed significantly as the company's recent demonstration flights in New York impressed investors and analysts. The company conducted historic point-to-point electric air taxi flights between JFK and Manhattan heliports, showcasing the aircraft's capabilities to regulators, Wall Street, and the public. These flights highlighted the aircraft's notably quiet acoustic signature compared to helicopters, addressing a key regulatory concern. JOBY stock ran from the high-$8s to above $11, with a close around $11.06 on May 13, 2026, representing a strong move of roughly 25 percent from late-April levels. The stock performance was driven by headlines about the successful demonstrations and the company's progress on multiple fronts. Recent announcements about the White House eIPP program and partnerships have also contributed to investor enthusiasm. The demonstrations showcase Joby's ability to operate electric air taxis safely and quietly in urban environments. Wall Street analysts have been impressed by the company's operational readiness and progress toward commercialization. The demonstrations also validate the technology's practical applicability for urban air mobility applications.
